message:
fix(scripts/pgo_build): gracefully fall back when perf LBR branch sampling is unavailable

The BOLT post-link optimization step previously assumed Linux `perf`
could always do LBR-based branch sampling (`-j any,u`). On hosts without
LBR (e.g. virtualized CPUs, nested KVM, some cloud shapes, kernels
built without branch-sample support, or CPUs that simply lack LBR), the
unconditional `perf record -j any,u` call silently produced a profile
that `perf2bolt` then rejected, breaking the whole PGO+BOLT pipeline
for anyone who isn't on bare metal Intel.

Now the script probes LBR availability with a trivial `perf record -j any,u
-- /bin/true` run against /dev/null. If that succeeds we use LBR as
before (the fastest, highest-fidelity path); if it fails we fall back
to basic sampling without `-j any,u` and pass `-nl` (no-LBR) to
`perf2bolt`, which is the documented BOLT workflow for non-LBR hosts.

The detected mode is logged at the BOLT stage so users reading
training logs can tell which code path the profile was captured on.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -221,9 +221,18 @@ if [[ "${RUN_BOLT}" -eq 1 ]]; then
         echo "[BOLT] Post-link optimization..."
         BOLT_PROFILE="${RUN_DIR}/bolt.fdata"
         BOLT_BIN="${PGO_BIN}.bolt"
-        perf record -e cycles:u -j any,u -o "${RUN_DIR}/perf.data" -- \
-            "${PGO_BIN}" "${BENCH_ARGV[@]}" > "${RUN_DIR}/bolt_training.log" 2>&1
-        perf2bolt -p "${RUN_DIR}/perf.data" -o "${BOLT_PROFILE}" "${PGO_BIN}"
+        if perf record -e cycles:u -j any,u -o /dev/null -- /bin/true 2>/dev/null; then
+            echo "      using LBR branch sampling"
+            perf record -e cycles:u -j any,u -o "${RUN_DIR}/perf.data" -- \
+                "${PGO_BIN}" "${BENCH_ARGV[@]}" > "${RUN_DIR}/bolt_training.log" 2>&1
+            PERF2BOLT_FLAGS=()
+        else
+            echo "      LBR unavailable; falling back to basic sampling (no-LBR mode)"
+            perf record -o "${RUN_DIR}/perf.data" -- \
+                "${PGO_BIN}" "${BENCH_ARGV[@]}" > "${RUN_DIR}/bolt_training.log" 2>&1
+            PERF2BOLT_FLAGS=(-nl)
+        fi
+        perf2bolt "${PERF2BOLT_FLAGS[@]}" -p "${RUN_DIR}/perf.data" -o "${BOLT_PROFILE}" "${PGO_BIN}"
         llvm-bolt "${PGO_BIN}" -o "${BOLT_BIN}" -data="${BOLT_PROFILE}" \
             -reorder-blocks=ext-tsp -reorder-functions=hfsort+ -split-functions -icf=1
         echo "      BOLT output: ${BOLT_BIN}"
